mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, causing equity investors to price in lower future cash flows and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or the cross-sectional correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns drops below -0.15 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer credit expansion accelerates while industrial production stagnates, it indicates demand-pull inflationary pressure that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, suppressing equity valuations over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (RSAFS growth minus INDPRO growth) shows no predictive power for 60-day forward returns (p > 0.10), or forward returns remain positive when the spread widens beyond historical 90th percentiles.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active rate hiking, a 10-basis-point increase in the federal funds rate compresses equity multiples by raising the risk-free rate component of the discount model, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during consecutive monthly rate hikes, or the coefficient on FEDFUNDS changes in a 60-day rolling regression flips sign from negative to positive.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real disposable income and consumer spending capacity drive higher corporate revenue growth expectations, which directly lift equity valuations and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The cross-sectional rank correlation between real wage growth (PAYEMS minus CPIAUCSL) and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are negative when real wages rise.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals supply chain bottlenecks and capacity constraints that erode corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances where INDPRO drops below its trend, or the predictive R-squared of the INDPRO deviation signal remains below 0.01 over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When non-farm payrolls growth exceeds 200,000 per month while housing starts decline, it indicates a labor-driven demand surge outpacing supply-side capacity, triggering inflation expectations that force monetary tightening and depress equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal (PAYEMS growth minus HOUST growth) fails to predict 60-day forward returns (p > 0.10), or forward returns remain positive when the labor-supply gap widens beyond historical norms.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary environment where CPI growth falls below 3.0 percent year-over-year, a month-over-month increase in housing starts signals resilient consumer confidence and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative or flat when HOUST rises alongside CPI below 3.0%, or the conditional forward return differential between rising HOUST and falling HOUST periods is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).
